volumeTitle: Birds. Lysistrata. Women at the Thesmophoria
label: Birds (Section 1.1496b - Section 1.1585)

sections.label: Section 1.1496b
sections.translation: Can you see any god behind me ?

sections.label: Section 1.1497b
sections.translation: No , none . But who are you , pray ?

sections.label: Section 1.1498b
sections.translation: What ' s the time , please ?

sections.label: Section 1.1499
sections.translation: The time ? Why , it ' s past noon .

sections.label: Section 1.1500
sections.translation: Who are you ?

sections.label: Section 1.1500b
sections.translation: Is it the fall of day ? Is it no later than that ?

sections.label: Section 1.1501
sections.translation: This is getting dull !

sections.label: Section 1.1501b
sections.translation: What is Zeus doing ? Is he dispersing the clouds or gathering them ?

sections.label: Section 1.1503
sections.translation: Watch out for yourself !

sections.label: Section 1.1503b
sections.translation: Come , I will raise my mask .

sections.label: Section 1.1504
sections.translation: Ah ! my dear Prometheus !

sections.label: Section 1.1504b
sections.translation: Sh ! Sh ! speak lower !

sections.label: Section 1.1505
sections.translation: Why , what ' s the matter , Prometheus ?

sections.label: Section 1.1505b
sections.translation: Sh ! sh ! Don ' t call me by my name ; you will be my ruin , if Zeus should see me here . But , if you want me to tell you how things are going in heaven , take this umbrella and shield me , so that the gods don ' t see me .

sections.label: Section 1.1510
sections.translation: I can recognize Prometheus in this cunning trick . Come , quick then , and fear nothing ; speak on .

sections.label: Section 1.1513
sections.translation: Then listen .

sections.label: Section 1.1513b
sections.translation: I am listening , proceed !

sections.label: Section 1.1514
sections.translation: Zeus is done for .

sections.label: Section 1.1514b
sections.translation: Ah ! and since when , pray ?

sections.label: Section 1.1515
sections.original: ἐξ οὗπερ ὑμεῖς ᾠκίσατε τὸν ἀέρα .
sections.translation: Since you founded this city in the air . There is not a man who now sacrifices to the gods ; the smoke of the victims no longer reaches us . Not the smallest offering comes ! We fast as though it were the festival of Demeter .

sections.label: Section 1.1520
sections.original: ἄνευ θυηλῶν · οἱ δὲ βάρβαροι θεοὶ
sections.translation: The barbarian gods , who are dying of hunger , are bawling like Illyrians and threaten to make an armed descent upon Zeus , if he does not open markets where joints of the victims are sold .

sections.label: Section 1.1525
sections.original: εἰσὶν γὰρ ἕτεροι βάρβαροι θεοί τινες
sections.translation: What ! there are other gods besides you , barbarian gods who dwell above Olympus ?

sections.label: Section 1.1526b
sections.translation: If there were no barbarian gods , who would be the patron of Execestides ?

sections.label: Section 1.1528
sections.original: ὄνομα δὲ τούτοις τοῖς θεοῖς τοῖς βαρβάροις
sections.translation: And what is the name of these gods ?

sections.label: Section 1.1529b
sections.translation: Their name ? Why , the Triballi .

sections.label: Section 1.1529c
sections.translation: Ah , indeed !

sections.label: Section 1.1530
sections.translation: ' tis from that no doubt that we derive the word ‘ tribulation .'

sections.label: Section 1.1531
sections.original: μάλιστα πάντων . ἓν δέ σοι λέγω σαφές ·
sections.translation: Most likely . But one thing I can tell you for certain , namely , that Zeus and the celestial Triballi are going to send deputies here to sue for peace . Now don ' t you treat with them , unless

sections.label: Section 1.1535
sections.original: τὸ σκῆπτρον ὁ Ζεὺς τοῖσιν ὄρνισιν πάλιν ,
sections.translation: Zeus restores the scepter to the birds and gives you Basileia in marriage .

sections.label: Section 1.1537
sections.translation: Who is this Basileia ?

sections.label: Section 1.1537b
sections.translation: A very fine young damsel , who makes the lightning for Zeus ; all things come from her ,

sections.label: Section 1.1540
sections.original: τὴν εὐνομίαν τὴν σωφροσύνην τὰ νεώρια ,
sections.translation: wisdom , good laws , virtue , the fleet , calumnies , the public paymaster and the triobolus .

sections.label: Section 1.1542
sections.translation: Ah ! then she is a sort of general manageress to the god .

sections.label: Section 1.1542b
sections.translation: Yes , precisely . If he gives you her for your wife , yours will be the almighty power . That is what I have come to tell you ;

sections.label: Section 1.1545
sections.original: ἀεί ποτʼ ἀνθρώποις γὰρ εὔνους εἴμʼ ἐγώ .
sections.translation: for you know my constant and habitual goodwill towards men .

sections.label: Section 1.1546
sections.original: μόνον θεῶν γὰρ διὰ σʼ ἀπανθρακίζομεν .
sections.translation: Oh , yes ! it ' s thanks to you that we roast our meat .

sections.label: Section 1.1547
sections.original: μισῶ δʼ ἅπαντας τοὺς θεούς , ὡς οἶσθα σύ .
sections.translation: I hate the gods , as you know .

sections.label: Section 1.1548
sections.original: νὴ τὸν Δίʼ ἀεὶ δῆτα θεομισὴς ἔφυς .
sections.translation: Aye , by Zeus , you have always detested them .

sections.label: Section 1.1549
sections.original: Τίμων καθαρός . ἀλλʼ ὡς ἂν ἀποτρέχω πάλιν ,
sections.translation: Towards them I am a veritable Timon ; but I must return in all haste ,

sections.label: Section 1.1550
sections.original: φέρε τὸ σκιάδειον , ἵνα με κἂν ὁ Ζεὺς ἴδῃ
sections.translation: so give me the umbrella ; if Zeus should see me from up there , he would think I was escorting one of the Canephori .

sections.label: Section 1.1552
sections.original: καὶ τὸν δίφρον γε διφροφόρει τονδὶ λαβών .
sections.translation: Wait , take this stool as well . Prometheus leaves . Pisthetaerus goes into the thicket .

sections.label: Section 1.1553
sections.translation: Near by the land of the Sciapodes there is a marsh , from the borders whereof the unwashed

sections.label: Section 1.1555
sections.translation: Socrates evokes the souls of men . Pisander came one day to see his soul , which he had left there when still alive . He offered

sections.label: Section 1.1560
sections.translation: a little victim , a camel , slit his throat and , following the example of Odysseus , stepped one pace backwards . Then that bat of a Chaerephon came up from hell to drink the camel ' s blood .

sections.label: Section 1.1565
sections.translation: This is the city of Nephelococcygia , to which we come as ambassadors . To Triballus . Hi ! what are you up to ? you are throwing your cloak over the left shoulder . Come , fling it quick over the right ! And why , pray , does it draggle in this fashion ? Have you ulcers to hide like Lespodias ?

sections.label: Section 1.1570
sections.original: ὦ δημοκρατία ποῖ προβιβᾷς ἡμᾶς ποτε ,
sections.translation: Oh ! democracy ! whither , oh ! whither are you leading us ? Is it possible that the gods have chosen such an envoy ?

sections.label: Section 1.1572
sections.translation: You are undisturbed ?

sections.label: Section 1.1572b
sections.original: οἴμωζε · πολὺ γὰρ δή σʼ ἐγὼ
sections.translation: Ugh ! you cursed savage ! you are by far the most barbarous of all the gods . —

sections.label: Section 1.1574
sections.translation: Tell me , Heracles , what are we going to do ?

sections.label: Section 1.1574b
sections.translation: I have already told you

sections.label: Section 1.1575
sections.original: ἐμοῦ γʼ ὅτι τὸν ἄνθρωπον ἄγχειν βούλομαι ,
sections.translation: that I want to strangle the fellow who dared to wall us out .

sections.label: Section 1.1577
sections.translation: But , my friend , we are envoys of peace .

sections.label: Section 1.1578b
sections.translation: All the more reason why I wish to strangle him .

sections.label: Section 1.1579
sections.original: τὴν τυρόκνηστίν τις δότω · φέρε σίλφιον ·
sections.translation: Hand me the cheese-grater ; bring me the silphium for sauce ;

sections.label: Section 1.1580
sections.original: τυρὸν φερέτω τις · πυρπόλει τοὺς ἄνθρακας .
sections.translation: pass me the cheese and watch the coals .

sections.label: Section 1.1581
sections.original: τὸν ἄνδρα χαίρειν οἱ θεοὶ κελεύομεν
sections.translation: Mortal ! we who greet you are three gods .

sections.label: Section 1.1582b
sections.translation: Wait a bit till I have prepared my silphium pickle .

sections.label: Section 1.1583
sections.original: τὰ δὲ κρέα τοῦ ταῦτʼ ἐστίν ;
sections.translation: What are these meats ?

sections.label: Section 1.1583b
sections.translation: These are birds that have been punished with death

sections.label: Section 1.1585
sections.translation: for attacking the people ' s friends .
